title: Questions about implementing a `nix-snapshotter` clone for Podman
body:
So, I am reimplementing [`nix-snapshotter`](https://github.com/pdtpartners/nix-snapshotter), which creates empty container layers with custom nix annotations to avoid any duplication of the Nix store for nix-built images through a containerd snapshotter, for Podman (https://github.com/pdtpartners/nix-snapshotter/issues/103), and I have ran into the following issue:
> Additional layer stores are only searched with a TOC annotation

This is annoying, as the way `nix-snapshotter` works is by replacing the layer diff with a layer that mounts a bunch of nix store stuff.
I have an LLM-generated fix for this that always checks additional layer stores first at https://github.com/bitbloxhub/container-libs/tree/als-first, and that seems to work. There was also an issue where the second run was erroring and thought the image was corrupt due to it being marked as a volatile layer, but that was also fixed, but I don't think that's the right solution?

comments.body: I guess this is related to #109 ? I don’t know what we want to do here… the storage backend plans are fairly unclear (#119 is not really a _plan_ but an indication of how far we are from one).
